revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox||{{High-use}}}} === Usage === This template adds __NOINDEX__, which may remove the page from some external search engines and automatically categorizes pages in [[:Category:Noindexed pages]]. This is sometimes done administratively {{crossref|(see {{section link|Wikipedia:User pages#Handling inappropriate content}})}}, but may also be done voluntarily by users to their own pages. By default, the template displays no content. *'''{{NOINDEX}}''' or *'''{{NOINDEX|visible=yes}}''' Parameters: * |visible = yes – makes the template display a line of plain text to inform editors that the page is excluded from search engine indexing: * |text = – customise the display text; defaults to "This page has been removed from search engines' indexes." === Effects in different namespaces === This template has no effect in the main (article) namespace unless the article is less than 90 days old. This is to prevent abuse. All articles which include this template are assigned to [[:Category:Noindexed articles]] regardless of their age (and whether they are actually [[noindex]]ed or not). This template has no effect in the Draft: or Draft Talk: namespaces, both of which are automatically noindexed via a software setting. === Warnings === '''Note:''' to avoid unexpected results or unnecessary duplication, read [[Wikipedia:Controlling search engine indexing]] to make sure you are familiar with the different ways noindexing is done. This template should not be used in articles. If an article is bad enough that search engines shouldn't index it, it should be nominated for deletion. __NOINDEX__ is disabled in [[WP:Main namespace|article space]] and this template consequently has no effect there. Note: major search engines should respect the NOINDEX tag, but it may take days or even weeks for content already indexed to be removed from them. === TemplateData === {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "description": "This template adds __NOINDEX__ to a page, a search engine tag that prevents indexing", "params": { "visible": { "label": "Visible", "description": "When set to 'yes', the template will display a message stating that the page is not indexed.", "type": "string", "required": false } } } === See also === * [[Template:INDEX]] {{When on basepage| [[Category:Typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Tracking templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia templates which apply NOINDEX]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox||{{High-use}}}} === Usage === This template adds __NOINDEX__, which may remove the page from some external search engines and automatically categorizes pages in [[:Category:Noindexed pages]]. This is sometimes done administratively {{crossref|(see {{section link|Wikipedia:User pages#Handling inappropriate content}})}}, but may also be done voluntarily by users to their own pages. By default, the template displays no content. *'''{{NOINDEX}}''' or *'''{{NOINDEX|visible=yes}}''' Parameters: * |visible = yes – makes the template display a line of plain text to inform editors that the page is excluded from search engine indexing: * |text = – customise the display text; defaults to "This page has been removed from search engines' indexes." === Effects in different namespaces === This template has no effect in the main (article) namespace unless the article is less than 90 days old. This is to prevent abuse. All articles which include this template are assigned to [[:Category:Noindexed articles]] regardless of their age (and whether they are actually [[noindex]]ed or not). This template has no effect in the Draft: or Draft Talk: namespaces, both of which are automatically noindexed via a software setting. === Warnings === '''Note:''' to avoid unexpected results or unnecessary duplication, read [[Wikipedia:Controlling search engine indexing]] to make sure you are familiar with the different ways noindexing is done. This template should not be used in articles. If an article is bad enough that search engines shouldn't index it, it should be nominated for deletion. __NOINDEX__ is disabled in [[WP:Main namespace|article space]] and this template consequently has no effect there. Note: major search engines should respect the NOINDEX tag, but it may take days or even weeks for content already indexed to be removed from them. === TemplateData === {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "description": "This template adds __NOINDEX__ to a page, a search engine tag that prevents indexing", "params": { "visible": { "label": "Visible", "description": "When set to 'yes', the template will display a message stating that the page is not indexed.", "type": "string", "required": false } } } === See also === * [[Template:INDEX]] {{When on basepage| [[Category:Typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Tracking templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia templates which apply NOINDEX]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox||{{High-use}}}} === Usage === This template adds __NOINDEX__, which may remove the page from some external search engines and automatically categorizes pages in [[:Category:Noindexed pages]]. This is sometimes done administratively {{crossref|(see {{section link|Wikipedia:User pages#Handling inappropriate content}})}}, but may also be done voluntarily by users to their own pages. By default, the template displays no content. *'''{{NOINDEX}}''' or *'''{{NOINDEX|visible=yes}}''' Parameters: * |visible = yes – makes the template display a line of plain text to inform editors that the page is excluded from search engine indexing: * |text = – customise the display text; defaults to "This page has been removed from search engines' indexes." === Effects in different namespaces === This template has no effect in the main (article) namespace unless the article is less than 90 days old. This is to prevent abuse. All articles which include this template are assigned to [[:Category:Noindexed articles]] regardless of their age (and whether they are actually [[noindex]]ed or not). This template has no effect in the Draft: or Draft Talk: namespaces, both of which are automatically noindexed via a software setting. === Warnings === '''Note:''' to avoid unexpected results or unnecessary duplication, read [[Wikipedia:Controlling search engine indexing]] to make sure you are familiar with the different ways noindexing is done. This template should not be used in articles. If an article is bad enough that search engines shouldn't index it, it should be nominated for deletion. __NOINDEX__ is disabled in [[WP:Main namespace|article space]] and this template consequently has no effect there. Note: major search engines should respect the NOINDEX tag, but it may take days or even weeks for content already indexed to be removed from them. === TemplateData === {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "description": "This template adds __NOINDEX__ to a page, a search engine tag that prevents indexing", "params": { "visible": { "label": "Visible", "description": "When set to 'yes', the template will display a message stating that the page is not indexed.", "type": "string", "required": false } } } === See also === * [[Template:INDEX]] {{When on basepage| [[Category:Typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Tracking templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia templates which apply NOINDEX]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox||{{High-use}}}} === Usage === This template adds __NOINDEX__, which may remove the page from some external search engines and automatically categorizes pages in [[:Category:Noindexed pages]]. This is sometimes done administratively {{crossref|(see {{section link|Wikipedia:User pages#Handling inappropriate content}})}}, but may also be done voluntarily by users to their own pages. By default, the template displays no content. *'''{{NOINDEX}}''' or *'''{{NOINDEX|visible=yes}}''' Parameters: * |visible = yes – makes the template display a line of plain text to inform editors that the page is excluded from search engine indexing: * |text = – customise the display text; defaults to "This page has been removed from search engines' indexes." === Effects in different namespaces === This template has no effect in the main (article) namespace unless the article is less than 90 days old. This is to prevent abuse. All articles which include this template are assigned to [[:Category:Noindexed articles]] regardless of their age (and whether they are actually [[noindex]]ed or not). This template has no effect in the Draft: or Draft Talk: namespaces, both of which are automatically noindexed via a software setting. === Warnings === '''Note:''' to avoid unexpected results or unnecessary duplication, read [[Wikipedia:Controlling search engine indexing]] to make sure you are familiar with the different ways noindexing is done. This template should not be used in articles. If an article is bad enough that search engines shouldn't index it, it should be nominated for deletion. __NOINDEX__ is disabled in [[WP:Main namespace|article space]] and this template consequently has no effect there. Note: major search engines should respect the NOINDEX tag, but it may take days or even weeks for content already indexed to be removed from them. === TemplateData === {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "description": "This template adds __NOINDEX__ to a page, a search engine tag that prevents indexing", "params": { "visible": { "label": "Visible", "description": "When set to 'yes', the template will display a message stating that the page is not indexed.", "type": "string", "required": false } } } === See also === * [[Template:INDEX]] {{When on basepage| [[Category:Typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Tracking templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia templates which apply NOINDEX]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 4 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23515730952|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox||{{High-use}}}} === Usage === This template adds __NOINDEX__, which may remove the page from some external search engines and automatically categorizes pages in [[:Category:Noindexed pages]]. This is sometimes done administratively {{crossref|(see {{section link|Wikipedia:User pages#Handling inappropriate content}})}}, but may also be done voluntarily by users to their own pages. By default, the template displays no content. *'''{{NOINDEX}}''' or *'''{{NOINDEX|visible=yes}}''' Parameters: * |visible = yes – makes the template display a line of plain text to inform editors that the page is excluded from search engine indexing: * |text = – customise the display text; defaults to "This page has been removed from search engines' indexes." === Effects in different namespaces === This template has no effect in the main (article) namespace unless the article is less than 90 days old. This is to prevent abuse. All articles which include this template are assigned to [[:Category:Noindexed articles]] regardless of their age (and whether they are actually [[noindex]]ed or not). This template has no effect in the Draft: or Draft Talk: namespaces, both of which are automatically noindexed via a software setting. === Warnings === '''Note:''' to avoid unexpected results or unnecessary duplication, read [[Wikipedia:Controlling search engine indexing]] to make sure you are familiar with the different ways noindexing is done. This template should not be used in articles. If an article is bad enough that search engines shouldn't index it, it should be nominated for deletion. __NOINDEX__ is disabled in [[WP:Main namespace|article space]] and this template consequently has no effect there. Note: major search engines should respect the NOINDEX tag, but it may take days or even weeks for content already indexed to be removed from them. === TemplateData === {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "description": "This template adds __NOINDEX__ to a page, a search engine tag that prevents indexing", "params": { "visible": { "label": "Visible", "description": "When set to 'yes', the template will display a message stating that the page is not indexed.", "type": "string", "required": false } } } === See also === * [[Template:INDEX]] {{When on basepage| }} [[Category:Typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Tracking templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia templates which apply NOINDEX]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24951476559|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{#ifeq:{{SUBPAGENAME}}|sandbox||{{High-use}}}} === Usage === This template adds __NOINDEX__, which may remove the page from some external search engines and automatically categorizes pages in [[:Category:Noindexed pages]]. This is sometimes done administratively {{crossref|(see {{section link|Wikipedia:User pages#Handling inappropriate content}})}}, but may also be done voluntarily by users to their own pages. By default, the template displays no content. *'''{{NOINDEX}}''' or *'''{{NOINDEX|visible=yes}}''' Parameters: * |visible = yes – makes the template display a line of plain text to inform editors that the page is excluded from search engine indexing: * |text = – customise the display text; defaults to "This page has been removed from search engines' indexes." === Effects in different namespaces === This template has no effect in the main (article) namespace unless the article is less than 90 days old. This is to prevent abuse. All articles which include this template are assigned to [[:Category:Noindexed articles]] regardless of their age (and whether they are actually [[noindex]]ed or not). This template has no effect in the Draft: or Draft Talk: namespaces, both of which are automatically noindexed via a software setting. === Warnings === '''Note:''' to avoid unexpected results or unnecessary duplication, read [[Wikipedia:Controlling search engine indexing]] to make sure you are familiar with the different ways noindexing is done. This template should not be used in articles. If an article is bad enough that search engines shouldn't index it, it should be nominated for deletion. __NOINDEX__ is disabled in [[WP:Main namespace|article space]] and this template consequently has no effect there. Note: major search engines should respect the NOINDEX tag, but it may take days or even weeks for content already indexed to be removed from them. === TemplateData === {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "description": "This template adds __NOINDEX__ to a page, a search engine tag that prevents indexing", "params": { "visible": { "label": "Visible", "description": "When set to 'yes', the template will display a message stating that the page is not indexed.", "type": "string", "required": false } } } === See also === * [[Template:INDEX]] {{When on basepage| }} [[Category:Typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Tracking templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia templates which apply NOINDEX]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
